pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh and verifier.py which use this path to write and read task results.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates this vendor and assigns it as the primary vendor for the affected products with pending purchase orders.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which generates these products with 'precision_id' as the primary vendor and sets up pending purchase orders for them.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which assigns these products to unaffected vendors (SkyTech or AeroAlloy) with no pending purchase orders from the disrupted vendor.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates confirmed purchase orders ('purchase' state) for these two products and verifier.py which penalizes the agent if these are modified.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which maps these exact backup vendors to the corresponding products during synthetic data generation.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which uses these exact values for the 'pending_po_qty' parameter when creating purchase orders for the affected products.
Confirmed via verifier.py logic where pass_threshold defaults to 80.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated Odoo inventory and procurement data. It is confirmed that the disrupted vendor 'Precision Aeroparts Inc.' has pending orders for products AERO-BRK-001 (qty 25), AERO-HYD-002 (qty 40), AERO-TRB-003 (qty 15), and AERO-FAS-004 (qty 100). The correct backup vendors explicitly configured in the environment are 'SkyTech Components Ltd.' for AERO-BRK-001 and AERO-HYD-002, and 'AeroAlloy Materials Corp.' for AERO-TRB-003 and AERO-FAS-004. Products AERO-CMP-006 and AERO-SEL-009 are correctly identified as having protected, active purchase orders from non-disrupted vendors that must not be altered.
